headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: content-security-policy
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The content-security-policy header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'content-security-policy'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
headers|missing_security_header|https://.remediation: Add a strict Content-Security-Policy. Start with default-src 'self' and tighten from there.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: x-frame-options
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The x-frame-options header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-frame-options'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: x-content-type-options
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The x-content-type-options header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-content-type-options'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: strict-transport-security
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The strict-transport-security header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'strict-transport-security'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
headers|missing_security_header|https://.remediation: Send `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` on HTTPS responses, then submit the domain to the HSTS preload list.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: x-xss-protection
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The x-xss-protection header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-xss-protection'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
headers|missing_security_header|https://.remediation: Set the x-xss-protection response header per OWASP guidance.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: referrer-policy
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The referrer-policy header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'referrer-policy'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.

headers|missing_security_header|https://.title: Confirm missing security header: permissions-policy
headers|missing_security_header|https://.description: The permissions-policy header is absent on this response.
steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers:
curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'permissions-policy'
2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
headers|missing_security_header|https://.remediation: Send a `Permissions-Policy` header explicitly disabling features your app doesn't need.
